name: RAP PAC is recruiting for rapalog dose and safety testing with no posted results
summary: RAP PAC repairs safety and pharmacology coverage for Rapalogs by tracking weekly sirolimus and everolimus dosing, but it remains no-results trial-watch context.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT05949658 as a recruiting 72-participant phase 1 study of weekly sirolimus or everolimus in adults aged 55 to 89, with dose-limiting toxicity, PK, mTOR signaling, omics, glucose tolerance, insulin-sensitivity, and safety endpoints and no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Adults aged 55 to 89 years free of overt chronic disease
time_horizon: Six-week intervention with follow-up visits over up to 17 weeks
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 72; estimated primary completion is March 2027; estimated study completion is December 2028; results were not posted.
caveats: Recruiting registry status is not evidence of benefit.
The study is phase 1 dose and pharmacology context rather than healthspan efficacy evidence.
Safety, pharmacokinetics, and mTOR target engagement are prerequisites for stronger claims but do not establish durable aging benefit alone.
